DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-14-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Raymond E. Kong, Esq. of Fisher & Kong LLP, Tel. 510.645.5880, for defendants and moving party;
J. Alber Kroemer, Esq. of Cantey Hanger LLP, Tel. 214.978.4167, for plaintiff and opposing party;
Wilma J. Gray, Esq. of McNamara Ney Beatty Slattery Borges & Ambacher LLP, Tel. 925.939.5330, for plaintiff and opposing party;
John R. Campo, Esq. of Branson Brinkop Griffith & Campo, Tel. 650.365.7710 x243, for defendant Old Republic, by Court Call.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS NIBBI BROS. INC., NIBBI BROS. ASSOCIATES, INC. and NIBBI BROS. ASSOCIATES, INC. DBA NIBBI CONCRETE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT as follows: The Defendants' Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the First, Third, and Fourth Causes of Action. Plaintiff failed to allege how it could amend the Complaint to cure the defects complained of in the Demurrer. Maxton v. Western States Metals (2012) 203 Cal.App.4th 81, 95 (burden is on the plaintiff to show that there is a reasonable possibility that the defect can be cured by amendment). The First Cause of Action alleging Breach of Contract and the Third Cause of Action for Express Indemnification fail to state facts sufficient to constitute causes of action as the exception included in the Contract's indemnity provision plainly states that the Defendants' duty to defend, indemnify, or hold harmless does not arise from acts caused by or omissions of the Plaintiff. (Complaint, Ex. A 20.) The underlying complaints in this action allege negligence as to the Plaintiff only because the employees are required to pursue worker's compensation claims against the Defendants. The acts and/or omissions for which Plaintiff seeks indemnity against arose from Plaintiff's alleged negligence and is barred by the plain language of the Contract. See C.J.L. Const., Inc. v. Univ. Plumbing (1993) 18 Cal.App.4th 376, 387. The Demurrer as to the Fourth Cause of Action for Declaratory Relief is sustained without leave to amend because Plaintiff has another form of relief adequate to determine their rights as contained in the Plaintiff's Sixteenth Affirmative Defense raised against both complaints in the underlying action. Calif. Ins. Guarantee Ass'n v. Super. Ct. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 1617, 1624. Defendants' Request for Judicial Notice is granted pursuant to Evidence Code 452(d). Plaintiff's Request for Judicial Notice is granted only as to the existence of the documents and not of the truth of the matters stated therein. See Glaski v. Bank of Am. (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 1079. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Sheryl L. Sawyer CSR# 5976.
